+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a080
+title: 'Spatial-nav debug overlay: blue zone border around `ui:navbar` and emerald scope borders around its buttons are missing in the running app'
+---
+## What
+
+Reproduced visually in `cargo tauri dev` with the spatial-nav debug overlay turned on (`<FocusDebugProvider enabled>` at `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:72`): the dashed borders that should mark the navbar zone and its three button scopes do **not** appear, even though debug overlays are visible elsewhere in the tree.
+
+Expected (per `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-debug-overlay.tsx:97-112`):
+
+- A **blue** dashed border (`border-blue-500/70`) hugging the entire navbar row, with a `zone:ui:navbar (x,y) w×h` label in the top-left.
+- An **emerald** dashed border (`border-emerald-500/70`) around each button's box, with a `scope:ui:navbar.{name} (x,y) w×h` label, for `ui:navbar.board-selector`, `ui:navbar.inspect`, and `ui:navbar.search`.
+
+Observed: neither the blue navbar-zone border nor the three emerald button-scope borders are visible in the running window. Other zones / scopes elsewhere in the tree (board, columns, perspective bar, cards) **do** render their overlays correctly under the same provider — so the gating flag and the overlay component are not globally broken; the issue is local to the navbar surface.
+
+## Where this lives
+
+- Navbar surface: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.tsx:67-162`
+  - Zone: `<FocusZone moniker="ui:navbar" showFocusBar={false} className="relative flex h-12 items-center border-b px-4 gap-2">` at line 79.
+  - Scopes: `ui:navbar.board-selector` (line 85), `ui:navbar.inspect` (line 97, gated on `board`), `ui:navbar.search` (line 133, `className="ml-auto"`).
+  - `<Field>` percent-complete (line 124-132) is its own peer zone — out of scope unless the same root cause applies.
+- Overlay component: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-debug-overlay.tsx` — `<span className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-50">` containing `<span className="absolute inset-0 border border-dashed ...">`.
+- Provider mount sites: `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:72` (main window) and `:127` (quick-capture). Both pass `enabled` so the flag is on for the running app.
+- Primitives that render the overlay:
+  - `<FocusZone>` `SpatialFocusZoneBody`: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx:560-562`.
+  - `<FocusScope>` `SpatialFocusScopeBody`: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:581-583`.
+
+The `relative` containing block both primitives need is merged in via `cn(consumerClassName, "relative")` at `focus-zone.tsx:540` and `focus-scope.tsx:553`. The navbar's outer FocusZone consumer className already includes `relative` explicitly — the merge is idempotent.
+
+## Hypotheses (test in this order)
+
+### H1 — Overlay paints at non-zero rect but is occluded by a sibling
+
+The navbar's `relative flex h-12 items-center border-b px-4 gap-2` builds a 48-px-tall row with a `border-b`. The progress-bar element (`nav-bar.tsx:151-159`) is `absolute bottom-0 left-0 right-0 h-0.5 overflow-hidden` and sits at `bottom-0` inside the same FocusZone. Its `<div>` child uses `bg-primary animate-indeterminate` — a **solid background**. If the progress bar's host has a high-enough effective z-context (via a transform / animation creating a stacking context) it could paint on top of the overlay and clip the dashed border at the bottom.
+
+But the navbar overlay is `z-50` on its outer span; the progress-bar host has no explicit z-index. Stacking-context evaluation under the FocusZone's `relative` parent should place `z-50` above `z-auto`. Verify in DevTools whether the progress bar is the occluder, especially while a command is in-flight. If yes, set `z-[60]` on the overlay or `z-40` on the progress bar — but prefer fixing the **root** layout interaction.
+
+### H2 — `inset-0` measures wrong against the navbar's `border-b`
+
+Tailwind defaults to `box-sizing: border-box`. `absolute inset-0` on a child positions to the **padding edge** of the offset parent. The dashed `border` (1 px) on the inner overlay span sits inside that padding edge. The host's own `border-b` paints on the bottom border edge — outside the overlay's box. So the overlay's bottom dashed line should land exactly at the inner edge of the host's border-b, **invisible** because the host's solid `border-b` paints over it from underneath at the same pixel row.
+
+If H2 alone explains it, only the bottom edge would be missing, not the full rectangle. The user's report says the **whole** navbar zone outline is missing. So this alone is unlikely to be the root cause — but verify it is not contributing.
+
+### H3 — FocusScope-around-Tooltip produces a zero-rect host
+
+`ui:navbar.inspect` (line 97) and `ui:navbar.search` (line 133) wrap a `<Tooltip>...<TooltipTrigger asChild><button/></TooltipTrigger>...</Tooltip>` block. The `<FocusScope>` renders a plain `<div>`. Inside a flex row, that `<div>` becomes a flex item; `display: block` shrinks to its in-flow content. `<Tooltip>` from Radix is a context provider with no DOM box; only `<TooltipTrigger asChild>` clones into the child `<button>`, and `<TooltipContent>` is portaled out. So the FocusScope div should size to ~the button.
+
+But: Radix's `<TooltipContent>` mounts a **portaled wrapper** even when closed in some configurations. If the tooltip provider renders any in-flow element inside the FocusScope div with `display: contents` or zero-rect inline content alongside the button, the FocusScope div may have unexpected layout. Confirm via a browser-mode test that snapshots `getBoundingClientRect()` on each FocusScope's host `<div>` at first paint and asserts non-zero width AND height. If the rect is zero-sized, `absolute inset-0` paints nothing visible.
+
+This hypothesis covers the **scope** half of the user's report (no emerald borders around the buttons). For `ui:navbar.board-selector` (line 85), the FocusScope wraps `<BoardSelector>` directly — same shape, but the BoardSelector's intrinsic content (button with the board name) is wider. If H3 is correct, the board-selector overlay might still not render if the FocusScope div is somehow zero-height.
+
+### H4 — Stacking context from `border-b` + the parent's `transform` / `overflow`
+
+The navbar lives inside `<div className="h-screen bg-background text-foreground flex flex-col overflow-hidden">` (`App.tsx:80`). `overflow-hidden` does not create a stacking context by itself. None of the ancestors that wrap NavBar (`BoardContainer`, `AppModeContainer`, `WindowContainer`, `RustEngineContainer`, `CommandBusyProvider`, `FocusLayer`'s debug-mode `<div className="relative">`) sets a `transform` or `filter` that would create one — except the FocusLayer's debug wrapper itself, which is `position: relative` only and so participates in normal stacking under z-50 children.
+
+Most likely **not** the culprit, but pin via DevTools `z-index` inspection.
+
+### H5 — `border-dashed` is being overridden by the ancestor's own border in the cascade
+
+`border border-dashed` produces `border-width: 1px; border-style: dashed;`. No global CSS in `kanban-app/ui/src/index.css` or shadcn's defaults overrides `border-style` on `<span>` elements. Quick eyeball check in DevTools: if the overlay span has `border-style: solid`, an ancestor stylesheet is winning the cascade. Unlikely but cheap to rule out.
+
+### H6 — The navbar zone's `<FocusDebugOverlay>` reports a zero rect
+
+The overlay reads its host rect via `hostRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` on every animation frame (`focus-debug-overlay.tsx:154-173`). The navbar zone's host is the FocusZone div; that div has `flex h-12 items-center` so it's a 48-px tall row spanning the full viewport width. Should be non-zero.
+
+But the **scope** divs (board-selector, inspect, search) are flex items with no explicit width — their rect is whatever the in-flow content collapses to. If the FocusScope's `<div>` somehow computes to zero width because the children are display:contents-style (Tooltip portaled out, button asChild), the overlay's `absolute inset-0` paints nothing.
+
+H3 and H6 are the same hypothesis viewed from two angles. Pin via the rect-snapshot test described in Tests below.
+
+## Approach
+
+### 1. Reproduce in a browser-mode test
+
+New file `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.focus-debug.browser.test.tsx`. Mount `<NavBar>` inside the production provider stack **with `<FocusDebugProvider enabled>`** at the top. Mirror the mock pattern from `nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx`. After first paint:
+
+- Assert the `[data-debug="zone"]` element exists as a child of the `[data-moniker="ui:navbar"]` host AND its bounding rect has `width >= 100` and `height >= 24` (the navbar should be at least the viewport-width minus padding by 48 px).
+- Assert each of the three `[data-debug="scope"]` elements (descended from `[data-moniker="ui:navbar.board-selector"]`, `[data-moniker="ui:navbar.inspect"]`, `[data-moniker="ui:navbar.search"]`) renders AND its bounding rect has `width >= 16` and `height >= 16` (each is a 24×24 button).
+- Assert each overlay's inner border span has `border-style: dashed` in computed style (catches H5).
+- Assert each overlay's outer span has computed `z-index: 50` and is the topmost element at its viewport coordinates (catches H1) — use `document.elementFromPoint(rect.x + 1, rect.y + 1)` and assert it is the overlay span or a descendant.
+
+This test fails before the fix and passes after.
+
+### 2. Identify the failing seam from DevTools + the test
+
+Run `cargo tauri dev`, open DevTools, inspect the navbar. Verify each of:
+
+- The FocusZone's `<div data-moniker="ui:navbar">` exists with `position: relative`.
+- A `<span data-debug="zone">` exists as its first child.
+- The `<span>`'s computed `border-style` is `dashed` (rules out H5).
+- The `<span>`'s `z-index` is 50 (rules out H1 — also confirm via `elementFromPoint`).
+- For the buttons: the FocusScope `<div data-moniker="ui:navbar.inspect">` has non-zero bounding rect (rules out H3/H6).
+
+The DevTools observations and the failing-test failure modes together pin the seam.
+
+### 3. Fix at the seam
+
+Likely candidates:
+
+- **If H3/H6**: the FocusScope `<div>` is zero-sized. Fix by ensuring the FocusScope wrapper has explicit `inline-flex items-center` (or `display: contents` is not in play). The navbar has `gap-2` so siblings already have a gap; the FocusScope's `<div>` just needs to size to its button child. Could add `className="inline-flex"` on the FocusScopes that wrap a single button. Verify pixel-faithful with the existing nav-bar layout tests.
+- **If H1**: `z-index` collision. Bump the overlay to `z-[60]` in `focus-debug-overlay.tsx`, or set the progress bar to `z-40`. Prefer keeping the overlay **above** because it's a debug aid and progress bars are production chrome.
+- **If H2**: shift the overlay's `inset-0` to `inset-[1px]` so the dashed border doesn't land directly on the host's `border-b`. Tiny bias, only matters if the dashed line is being optically eaten by the host's solid border.
+- **If H5**: stylesheet overrides — find and rein in.
+
+Do not fix more than the failing test points at.
+
+### 4. Regression guard
+
+Keep the new browser-mode test in the repo as the regression guard so a future change to the navbar layout (or to the overlay component) cannot silently re-break the visual debug aid.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+All asserted by automated tests below — no manual smoke step.
+
+- [ ] In the running app with debug enabled, a blue dashed `[data-debug="zone"]` border is visible around the entire navbar row.
+- [ ] Each of `ui:navbar.board-selector`, `ui:navbar.inspect`, `ui:navbar.search` shows an emerald dashed `[data-debug="scope"]` border around its button.
+- [ ] Each overlay's coordinate label (top-left) reads non-zero `(x, y)` and non-zero `width × height`.
+- [ ] No regression on click handling: clicking each navbar button still dispatches the existing command (board-selector popover, `ui.inspect`, `app.search`). Pre-existing tests in `nav-bar.spatial-nav.test.tsx` keep passing.
+- [ ] No regression on focus indicator: the `<FocusIndicator>` still renders on the focused leaf. Pre-existing tests in `nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx` keep passing.
+- [ ] No regression on the percent-complete `<Field>` zone — its overlay renders too (sanity check that this fix doesn't break the peer zone next door).
+
+## Tests
+
+All tests are automated. No manual verification.
+
+### `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.focus-debug.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+- [ ] `navbar_zone_renders_blue_dashed_overlay` — mount NavBar inside `<FocusDebugProvider enabled>` + spatial provider stack; assert `[data-moniker="ui:navbar"] > [data-debug="zone"]` exists, has `border-style: dashed` and `border-color` matching `border-blue-500/70`, and its bounding rect width × height is non-zero (>= 100 × 24).
+- [ ] `navbar_button_scopes_render_emerald_dashed_overlays` — same harness; assert each of the three `[data-moniker="ui:navbar.{board-selector,inspect,search}"]` hosts contains a `[data-debug="scope"]` child, dashed, emerald, with non-zero bounding rect (>= 16 × 16).
+- [ ] `navbar_overlay_is_topmost_at_its_coordinates` — pick a point inside the navbar overlay's rect; `document.elementFromPoint(x+1, y+1)` returns the overlay span (or a descendant), proving `z-50` is not occluded by the progress bar or any sibling.
+- [ ] `navbar_overlay_inner_border_renders_when_progress_bar_is_active` — same as the topmost test but with the in-flight counter set to a non-zero value so the progress bar is mounted (`isBusy === true`); assert the overlay still wins the topmost check. Regression guard for H1.
+- [ ] `navbar_button_scope_rect_is_non_zero_for_tooltip_wrapped_buttons` — mount NavBar; for each of inspect and search (the Tooltip-wrapped buttons), assert the FocusScope `<div data-moniker=…>` has bounding rect width AND height greater than 0. Regression guard for H3/H6.
+
+Test command: `cd kanban-app/ui && bun test nav-bar.focus-debug.browser` — all five pass.
+
+### Existing tests must keep passing
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.spatial-nav.test.tsx` — click and arrow-nav semantics unchanged.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx` — focus indicator wiring unchanged.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-debug-overlay.browser.test.tsx` — overlay component contract unchanged.
+
+Test command: `cd kanban-app/ui && bun test nav-bar focus-debug-overlay` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the five failing browser-mode assertions first against the production wiring, then run the app in DevTools to identify which hypothesis the failures match, then fix only the seam the failing tests point at.
+- Do **not** modify `<FocusDebugOverlay>` or `<FocusZone>` / `<FocusScope>` primitives unless the failing test localises the bug there. The fix is most likely a NavBar-local layout adjustment.
+
+#frontend #spatial-nav #kanban-app
\ No newline at end of file

## What

Reproduced visually in `cargo tauri dev` with the spatial-nav debug overlay turned on (`<FocusDebugProvider enabled>` at `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:72`): the dashed borders that should mark the navbar zone and its three button scopes do **not** appear, even though debug overlays are visible elsewhere in the tree.

Expected (per `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-debug-overlay.tsx:97-112`):

- A **blue** dashed border (`border-blue-500/70`) hugging the entire navbar row, with a `zone:ui:navbar (x,y) w×h` label in the top-left.
- An **emerald** dashed border (`border-emerald-500/70`) around each button's box, with a `scope:ui:navbar.{name} (x,y) w×h` label, for `ui:navbar.board-selector`, `ui:navbar.inspect`, and `ui:navbar.search`.

Observed: neither the blue navbar-zone border nor the three emerald button-scope borders are visible in the running window. Other zones / scopes elsewhere in the tree (board, columns, perspective bar, cards) **do** render their overlays correctly under the same provider — so the gating flag and the overlay component are not globally broken; the issue is local to the navbar surface.

## Where this lives

- Navbar surface: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.tsx:67-162`
  - Zone: `<FocusZone moniker="ui:navbar" showFocusBar={false} className="relative flex h-12 items-center border-b px-4 gap-2">` at line 79.
  - Scopes: `ui:navbar.board-selector` (line 85), `ui:navbar.inspect` (line 97, gated on `board`), `ui:navbar.search` (line 133, `className="ml-auto"`).
  - `<Field>` percent-complete (line 124-132) is its own peer zone — out of scope unless the same root cause applies.
- Overlay component: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-debug-overlay.tsx` — `<span className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-50">` containing `<span className="absolute inset-0 border border-dashed ...">`.
- Provider mount sites: `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:72` (main window) and `:127` (quick-capture). Both pass `enabled` so the flag is on for the running app.
- Primitives that render the overlay:
  - `<FocusZone>` `SpatialFocusZoneBody`: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx:560-562`.
  - `<FocusScope>` `SpatialFocusScopeBody`: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx:581-583`.

The `relative` containing block both primitives need is merged in via `cn(consumerClassName, "relative")` at `focus-zone.tsx:540` and `focus-scope.tsx:553`. The navbar's outer FocusZone consumer className already includes `relative` explicitly — the merge is idempotent.

## Hypotheses (test in this order)

### H1 — Overlay paints at non-zero rect but is occluded by a sibling

The navbar's `relative flex h-12 items-center border-b px-4 gap-2` builds a 48-px-tall row with a `border-b`. The progress-bar element (`nav-bar.tsx:151-159`) is `absolute bottom-0 left-0 right-0 h-0.5 overflow-hidden` and sits at `bottom-0` inside the same FocusZone. Its `<div>` child uses `bg-primary animate-indeterminate` — a **solid background**. If the progress bar's host has a high-enough effective z-context (via a transform / animation creating a stacking context) it could paint on top of the overlay and clip the dashed border at the bottom.

But the navbar overlay is `z-50` on its outer span; the progress-bar host has no explicit z-index. Stacking-context evaluation under the FocusZone's `relative` parent should place `z-50` above `z-auto`. Verify in DevTools whether the progress bar is the occluder, especially while a command is in-flight. If yes, set `z-[60]` on the overlay or `z-40` on the progress bar — but prefer fixing the **root** layout interaction.

### H2 — `inset-0` measures wrong against the navbar's `border-b`

Tailwind defaults to `box-sizing: border-box`. `absolute inset-0` on a child positions to the **padding edge** of the offset parent. The dashed `border` (1 px) on the inner overlay span sits inside that padding edge. The host's own `border-b` paints on the bottom border edge — outside the overlay's box. So the overlay's bottom dashed line should land exactly at the inner edge of the host's border-b, **invisible** because the host's solid `border-b` paints over it from underneath at the same pixel row.

If H2 alone explains it, only the bottom edge would be missing, not the full rectangle. The user's report says the **whole** navbar zone outline is missing. So this alone is unlikely to be the root cause — but verify it is not contributing.

### H3 — FocusScope-around-Tooltip produces a zero-rect host

`ui:navbar.inspect` (line 97) and `ui:navbar.search` (line 133) wrap a `<Tooltip>...<TooltipTrigger asChild><button/></TooltipTrigger>...</Tooltip>` block. The `<FocusScope>` renders a plain `<div>`. Inside a flex row, that `<div>` becomes a flex item; `display: block` shrinks to its in-flow content. `<Tooltip>` from Radix is a context provider with no DOM box; only `<TooltipTrigger asChild>` clones into the child `<button>`, and `<TooltipContent>` is portaled out. So the FocusScope div should size to ~the button.

But: Radix's `<TooltipContent>` mounts a **portaled wrapper** even when closed in some configurations. If the tooltip provider renders any in-flow element inside the FocusScope div with `display: contents` or zero-rect inline content alongside the button, the FocusScope div may have unexpected layout. Confirm via a browser-mode test that snapshots `getBoundingClientRect()` on each FocusScope's host `<div>` at first paint and asserts non-zero width AND height. If the rect is zero-sized, `absolute inset-0` paints nothing visible.

This hypothesis covers the **scope** half of the user's report (no emerald borders around the buttons). For `ui:navbar.board-selector` (line 85), the FocusScope wraps `<BoardSelector>` directly — same shape, but the BoardSelector's intrinsic content (button with the board name) is wider. If H3 is correct, the board-selector overlay might still not render if the FocusScope div is somehow zero-height.

### H4 — Stacking context from `border-b` + the parent's `transform` / `overflow`

The navbar lives inside `<div className="h-screen bg-background text-foreground flex flex-col overflow-hidden">` (`App.tsx:80`). `overflow-hidden` does not create a stacking context by itself. None of the ancestors that wrap NavBar (`BoardContainer`, `AppModeContainer`, `WindowContainer`, `RustEngineContainer`, `CommandBusyProvider`, `FocusLayer`'s debug-mode `<div className="relative">`) sets a `transform` or `filter` that would create one — except the FocusLayer's debug wrapper itself, which is `position: relative` only and so participates in normal stacking under z-50 children.

Most likely **not** the culprit, but pin via DevTools `z-index` inspection.

### H5 — `border-dashed` is being overridden by the ancestor's own border in the cascade

`border border-dashed` produces `border-width: 1px; border-style: dashed;`. No global CSS in `kanban-app/ui/src/index.css` or shadcn's defaults overrides `border-style` on `<span>` elements. Quick eyeball check in DevTools: if the overlay span has `border-style: solid`, an ancestor stylesheet is winning the cascade. Unlikely but cheap to rule out.

### H6 — The navbar zone's `<FocusDebugOverlay>` reports a zero rect

The overlay reads its host rect via `hostRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` on every animation frame (`focus-debug-overlay.tsx:154-173`). The navbar zone's host is the FocusZone div; that div has `flex h-12 items-center` so it's a 48-px tall row spanning the full viewport width. Should be non-zero.

But the **scope** divs (board-selector, inspect, search) are flex items with no explicit width — their rect is whatever the in-flow content collapses to. If the FocusScope's `<div>` somehow computes to zero width because the children are display:contents-style (Tooltip portaled out, button asChild), the overlay's `absolute inset-0` paints nothing.

H3 and H6 are the same hypothesis viewed from two angles. Pin via the rect-snapshot test described in Tests below.

## Approach

### 1. Reproduce in a browser-mode test

New file `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.focus-debug.browser.test.tsx`. Mount `<NavBar>` inside the production provider stack **with `<FocusDebugProvider enabled>`** at the top. Mirror the mock pattern from `nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx`. After first paint:

- Assert the `[data-debug="zone"]` element exists as a child of the `[data-moniker="ui:navbar"]` host AND its bounding rect has `width >= 100` and `height >= 24` (the navbar should be at least the viewport-width minus padding by 48 px).

- Assert each overlay's inner border span has `border-style: dashed` in computed style (catches H5).
- Assert each overlay's outer span has computed `z-index: 50` and is the topmost element at its viewport coordinates (catches H1) — use `document.elementFromPoint(rect.x + 1, rect.y + 1)` and assert it is the overlay span or a descendant.

This test fails before the fix and passes after.

### 2. Identify the failing seam from DevTools + the test

Run `cargo tauri dev`, open DevTools, inspect the navbar. Verify each of:

- The FocusZone's `<div data-moniker="ui:navbar">` exists with `position: relative`.
- A `<span data-debug="zone">` exists as its first child.
- The `<span>`'s computed `border-style` is `dashed` (rules out H5).
- The `<span>`'s `z-index` is 50 (rules out H1 — also confirm via `elementFromPoint`).
- For the buttons: the FocusScope `<div data-moniker="ui:navbar.inspect">` has non-zero bounding rect (rules out H3/H6).

The DevTools observations and the failing-test failure modes together pin the seam.

### 3. Fix at the seam

Likely candidates:

- **If H3/H6**: the FocusScope `<div>` is zero-sized. Fix by ensuring the FocusScope wrapper has explicit `inline-flex items-center` (or `display: contents` is not in play). The navbar has `gap-2` so siblings already have a gap; the FocusScope's `<div>` just needs to size to its button child. Could add `className="inline-flex"` on the FocusScopes that wrap a single button. Verify pixel-faithful with the existing nav-bar layout tests.
- **If H1**: `z-index` collision. Bump the overlay to `z-[60]` in `focus-debug-overlay.tsx`, or set the progress bar to `z-40`. Prefer keeping the overlay **above** because it's a debug aid and progress bars are production chrome.

- **If H5**: stylesheet overrides — find and rein in.

Do not fix more than the failing test points at.

### 4. Regression guard

Keep the new browser-mode test in the repo as the regression guard so a future change to the navbar layout (or to the overlay component) cannot silently re-break the visual debug aid.

## What

**Release blocker.** The navbar in the running app is non-functional for keyboard users:

1. **No visible focus indicator** on any navbar leaf when keyboard focus arrives there. The user sees no cursor-bar next to the board-selector, the inspect button, the percent-complete field, or the search button — even though the kernel reports those leaves as registered (the per-test assertions in `nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx` pass against an isolated mount).
2. **Arrow keys do not traverse the navbar.** The user cannot Left/Right between navbar entries. They cannot Up from the board into the navbar, nor Down from the navbar into the perspective bar / board.
3. **Debug overlay missing too** (the original symptom). When `<FocusDebugProvider enabled>` is mounted at `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:72`, no blue dashed border appears around the navbar zone and no emerald borders appear around the navbar leaves — even though overlays render correctly elsewhere in the same provider tree.

All three symptoms point to the **same underlying break** localised to the navbar surface. The kernel-side registration is happening (the spatial-nav test mocks confirm `spatial_register_zone` / `spatial_register_scope` are invoked at first paint), and the per-component browser-mode tests in `nav-bar.focus-indicator.browser.test.tsx` and `navbar_arrow_nav.rs` pass — but the production tree is broken. Either:

- The production provider stack differs from the isolated test harness in a way that's load-bearing (a missing context, a different layer parent, an HMR-only edge), OR
- The kernel's focus-claim subscription on the navbar leaves doesn't reach React state in production (the `useFocusClaim` callback never fires the `setFocused` it gets handed), OR
- The navbar's spatial registration silently lands on the wrong layer / parent zone in production (so beam-search filters it out and the indicator's claim subscription is keyed to nothing), OR
- A recent change broke an end-to-end seam that the unit-level tests don't exercise.

The earlier card `01KQ9XWHP2Y5H1QB5B3RJFEBBR` was marked done because its tests passed — but its tests don't reproduce the production failure mode. **That card's "regression guards" are insufficient.** This card is the production-tree fix.

## Where this lives

- Navbar surface: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/nav-bar.tsx:67-162`
  - Zone: `<FocusZone moniker="ui:navbar" showFocusBar={false} className="relative flex h-12 items-center border-b px-4 gap-2">` at line 79.
  - Scopes: `ui:navbar.board-selector` (line 85), `ui:navbar.inspect` (line 97, gated on `board`), `ui:navbar.search` (line 133, `className="ml-auto"`).
  - Field zone (peer of leaves): `<Field>` percent-complete at lines 124-132 — registered as `field:board:<id>.percent_complete`.
- Provider stack: `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx:69-104` — `DiagErrorBoundary` → `FocusDebugProvider enabled` → `SpatialFocusProvider` → `FocusLayer name="window"` → `CommandBusyProvider` → `RustEngineContainer` → `WindowContainer` → `AppModeContainer` → `BoardContainer` → `<div className="h-screen ...flex flex-col overflow-hidden">` → `<NavBar />`.
- Spatial primitives: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx`, `focus-layer.tsx`, `focus-indicator.tsx`, `focus-debug-overlay.tsx`.
- Focus-claim subscription: `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx` — `useFocusClaim(key, setFocused)` is the seam that drives `data-focused` and the `<FocusIndicator>` render. If the per-key callback never fires, the indicator never mounts.

## Hypotheses (test in this order — root cause first)

### H1 — Production tree mounts the navbar **outside** the window-root layer

The navbar is rendered inside `BoardContainer` → `<div>` at `App.tsx:80`. `BoardContainer` is a conditional render (loading / empty / active) that may, depending on the board state, render its children inside a different inner subtree. Verify the `<NavBar>` is reached by descendants of the `<FocusLayer name="window">` push at App.tsx:74 in **every** board state. If `BoardContainer` short-circuits (e.g. an empty-state branch that renders the navbar in a fallback that is NOT a child of FocusLayer's context provider), the navbar's `useContext(FocusLayerContext)` returns `null`, the FocusZone falls through to `FallbackFocusZoneBody` (`focus-zone.tsx:613-700`), and the spatial registration is **skipped entirely** — no kernel registration, no claim subscription, no indicator, no debug overlay, no arrow nav. Tests don't catch this because they mount NavBar inside an explicit `<FocusLayer>` wrapper.

This is the most likely root cause.

### H2 — `FocusDebugProvider`'s `<div className="relative">` wrap (when debug is on) breaks an ancestor ref / context

`focus-layer.tsx:200-204` wraps the entire layer body in a `<div className="relative">` when `useFocusDebug()` returns `true`. That wrapper sits between the `FocusLayerContext.Provider` and its descendants in the DOM but **not** in React tree, so context propagation is unaffected. But: the wrapper changes the offset-parent chain, the scrollable-ancestor chain, and the React Profiler's component tree. If anything in `useTrackRectOnAncestorScroll` or the focus-claim subscription depends on a particular ancestor, the wrapper could break it. Toggle debug off in App.tsx (set `enabled={false}`) and verify the navbar focus indicator + arrow nav come back. If yes, this hypothesis is alive — narrow further.

### H3 — `useFocusClaim` callback never fires for navbar keys in production

The kernel emits `focus-changed` events; `useFocusClaim` subscribes per `SpatialKey` and calls `setFocused`. If the navbar's keys are registered but the `focus-changed` listener filter (e.g. by layer key) excludes them, the callback never fires. Verify by adding a `console.log` in the navbar `<FocusScope>` body's `useFocusClaim(key, setFocused)` site and observing whether it ever logs in the running app when the user clicks a navbar button. The isolated test mocks `listen("focus-changed", cb)` and replays events — it doesn't exercise the same end-to-end path.

### H4 — A recent change moved the navbar out of the keyboard-event-handling layer

The arrow-key router (Rust kernel + React adapter) routes keys based on the active layer. If the active layer when focus is anywhere on the board is the inspector or some other modal layer, arrows route there and the navbar is unreachable. Verify the active layer in production via DevTools (or a debug overlay toggle that prints the active layer key).

### H5 — Production-only HMR / suspense / portal artifact

Vite HMR or React 19 suspense can re-mount components in a way that orphans subscriptions. If the FocusZone for the navbar mounts before its `<FocusLayer>` finishes pushing its layer (a race the test mocks paper over by resolving `pushLayer` synchronously), the registration could land with a stale `parent` and beam-search wouldn't find it. Pin via a test that delays `pushLayer` resolution and asserts the navbar still ends up registered under the layer key.

### H6 — Original symptom (debug overlay only)

Even after H1–H5 are fixed, the debug-overlay-specific symptoms (occlusion / zero-rect / cascade override) from the original task hypotheses still need a quick eyeball check. They become irrelevant if H1–H5 already explains all three symptoms (no registration → no claim → no indicator → no overlay).
